titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Douglas ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Douglas ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 2789. RumboldSir Horace Anthony Rumbold, Head of the Southern Department, British Foreign Office. informed us Foreign Office instructed British Ambassador Athens to urge upon Greek Government that it endeavor to avoid further Yugoslav frontier incidents. He indicated that this was result of Bevin’s concern this regard and British desire that “in long run Greek and Yugoslav Government get on.” According Rumbold Pipinelis assured British Ambassador Greeks would endeavor prevent further incidents.

Rumbold said Foreign Office has now also instructed British Ambassador Belgrade to urge Yugoslavs on appropriate occasion not to
exaggerate Greek-Yugoslav frontier incidents but to discuss such matters with Greeks along lines of meeting re Skočiver incident.Regarding the meeting on the Skočivir incident, see footnote 5 to telegram 1335, July 12, from Athens, p. 367.
